patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/443

## Problem
The structured error envelope carries two constant-prose fields on every error (`required`: "Use values that match documented command constraints."; `why`: "Validation protects data consistency..."). They never vary by error and carry no actionable signal — pure token overhead in retry loops.

## Proposed fix
Lean error mode (or simply omit constant `why`/`required` under --json unless error-specific): keep `code`, `detail`, `examples`, `next_steps`, `recovery.suggested_retry`. Verbose prose stays for human/TTY.

## Definition of done
JSON errors drop generic boilerplate (or --lean-errors/PM_LEAN_ERRORS added); error-envelope spec updated; agent docs note the mode. Related: pm-gdi7 covers unknown-option did-you-mean in the same envelope.
GH-443: lean error mode — drop constant required/why boilerplate from structured errors

Dogfood GH-443: every structured error carries two constant prose fields (required/why) that never vary by error; keep only actionable fields in lean mode. Duplicate check: pm-gdi7 covers unknown-option suggestions, not envelope size; no other overlap.
JSON errors omit generic constant prose while keeping code/detail/examples/next_steps/recovery; error-envelope spec updated; mode documented for agents

author: claude-code-agent
message: Release claim after documenting current bundle progress; not closing unresolved scope.
